authoritative_sources.title: Final Rule Defining Larger Participants of a Market for General-Use Digital Consumer Payment Applications
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative CFPB rulemaking defining payment wallet functionality, digital payment application scope, and embedded-finance delivery context for wallet providers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: wallet scope definition, terminology normalization, and embedded-finance boundary setting
used_for: defining what counts as a wallet-specific workflow
distinguishing wallet operations from generic mobile app support
framing embedded-finance and third-party delivery models
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to constrain intake, terminology, and boundary scenarios so the pack requests payment-wallet facts instead of generic application support facts.

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR Part 1005, Electronic Fund Transfers (Regulation E)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. regulation governing electronic fund transfer and prepaid-account protections that shape wallet incident and customer-impact handling boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer-impact review, incident severity framing, and completion gating when EFT or prepaid facts matter
used_for: refusal triggers when legal interpretation would be required
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to keep the lane from claiming regulated determinations when a wallet incident intersects EFT or prepaid-account obligations.

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers FAQs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB guidance clarifying how electronic fund transfer requirements apply in digital wallet and prepaid contexts common in fintech operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: wallet product context review and customer-impact escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used in scenarios that require wallet-specific facts before completion or customer-impact statements can be packaged.

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC IT Examination Handbook Appendix E: Mobile Financial Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC mobile-financial-services guidance covering risk identification, measurement, mitigation, monitoring, and third-party management for mobile wallets and related payment channels.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: incident triage, dependency identification, access risk review, and monitoring expectations
used_for: incident and access workflow differentiation from generic IT operations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to define wallet-system failure modes, third-party interface dependencies, and monitoring-driven exception handling.

authoritative_sources.title: EMV Payment Tokenisation: A Guide to Use Cases v2.2.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment-industry workflow reference defining token-program roles, token issuance and provisioning, token processing, wallet use cases, and payment-token lifecycle events that materially govern digital wallet operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: token issuance, provisioning, presentment, processing, and lifecycle management
used_for: change and incident workflow design tied to token operations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to make the pack request token requestor, token service provider, wallet, processor, and lifecycle event artifacts instead of generic application logs alone.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3, Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public incident-response workflow reference used to constrain incident triage, containment, escalation, and recovery handling for wallet-platform outages and security-sensitive operational events.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: incident triage, escalation, recovery, and closure readiness
used_for: completion gating when recovery evidence is incomplete
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to keep incident handling explicit and reversible when wallet security or service restoration evidence remains incomplete.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-63B, Authentication and Authenticator Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public authentication workflow reference for operator access, authenticator events, and lifecycle controls relevant to wallet access readiness and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privileged-access readiness and authentication evidence review
used_for: refusal boundaries for unsupported access approval
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to distinguish access-evidence validation from actual access approval and to require named authenticator and approval records for privileged operations.

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment security control standard defining baseline technical and operational requirements for protecting payment account data and audit evidence in card-enabled wallet environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: change-readiness, logging, access control evidence, and audit-ready closure
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to require named control evidence for access-sensitive or card-data-sensitive wallet work instead of generic 'change complete' claims.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and wallet context verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate wallet product model, payment rail, sponsor-bank or processor context, systems of record, and ticket ownership before any lifecycle movement.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Incident, access, and dependency evidence normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize wallet platform, token lifecycle, ledger, processor, IAM, and customer-impact artifacts so the request has a consistent evidence bundle and authoritative record chain.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution-state progression and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Move work through queued, under-review, blocked, escalated, rework, ready-for-completion, and completed states based on wallet evidence, dependency status, and approval boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Change-readiness and interface risk review
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate release evidence, rollback readiness, access posture, and cross-system dependencies before recommending wallet change progression or hold.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion records only when incident recovery, dependency restoration, change validation, and required owner or customer-impact evidence are present.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Wallet operations tickets with lifecycle state, severity, and requested action
Wallet platform records for token provisioning, enrollment, device binding, suspend or resume, deletion, and wallet ledger linkage
Issuer processor, token service provider, card-network, payment gateway, and settlement acknowledgements
Identity and access management logs, privileged-role approvals, authenticator events, and service account records
Change tickets with testing evidence, rollback plans, approval notes, and release windows
Customer-impact logs, notification status, and partner escalation notes
Exception queue notes, dependency owner routing packages, and completion evidence records
systems_of_record: Wallet operations case-management or ITSM system
Wallet platform or wallet ledger system
Token management or token service provider portal
Issuer processor, sponsor-bank, payment gateway, and card-network operations systems
Identity and access management systems and authentication telemetry
Change management and release control systems
Customer communication, notification, and incident-status systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on digital wallet operations ownership.
Validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required for safe wallet-case progression.
Prepare execution status updates, system incident summaries, exception notes, dependency routing notes, and completion evidence records tied to named artifacts.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
escalate: Missing or contradictory token, ledger, processor, sponsor-bank, or tenant-policy evidence materially changes the outcome.
Fraud, AML, disputes, reimbursement, legal, PCI exception, security exception, or approval authority is required.
Privileged-access decisions, release approvals, or network and processor overrides leave the lane's operational authority.
Wallet platform and processor or token-program records disagree on current state or closure readiness.
refuse: Requests to fabricate approvals, regulated interpretations, access grants, release signoff, or completion evidence.
Requests that require legal, compliance, fraud, security engineering, sponsor-bank, or network authority outside the lane.
Requests to mark work complete while recovery evidence, rollback proof, or required owner evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a generic mobile app incident as sufficient evidence for a digital wallet operations decision without token, ledger, processor, or payment-rail context.
Advancing wallet work when token lifecycle state, processor status, and wallet ledger status do not reconcile.
Allowing privileged-access or change-readiness recommendations to imply approval authority that the lane does not own.
Masking sponsor-bank, network, or processor dependency failures behind generic incident closure language.
Closing wallet work without traceable recovery evidence, customer-impact handling, or required rollback and approval artifacts.
Applying public baseline guidance when tenant sponsor-bank, processor, or network controls require stricter holds, windows, or approvals.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether the controlling record of truth for wallet lifecycle state is the wallet platform, token service provider, processor, or internal ledger for a given tenant program
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant system-of-record precedence rules before high-impact state changes or completion packaging.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether the tenant wallet is open loop, closed loop, prepaid, or embedded pass-through credential storage and which sponsor-bank controls therefore apply
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant product model and sponsor-bank program context before customer-impact statements or completion claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Which tenant approval owner controls privileged access, release signoff, and network or processor maintenance windows for the affected wallet workflow
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require approval maps and escalation roster before advancing access-sensitive or release-sensitive work.
